items.text: Field Value Chapter GovTech Maturity Variable Id govtech-startup-48.5 Definition Capacity of Government to deliver online services via PPPs Format 0=Unknown/n.a;1=No PPP for online services 2=Yes;PPP arrangements exist for online service delivery Other Metadata

items.title: SME support
items.text: Field Value Chapter GovTech Maturity Variable Id govtech-startup-48.6 Definition Is there a procurement policy aimed at prioritizing bids from startups/SMEs? (e.g., having a quota for SMEs) Format 0=Unknown/n.a;1=No;2=Yes Other Metadata

items.title: Transparency
items.text: Field Value Chapter GovTech Maturity Variable Id govtech-startup-48.7 Definition Publishing of the results/progress in supporting startups/SMEs for innovation? Format 0=Unknown/n.a;1=No;2=Yes (internal;not published); 3=Yes (public;published) Other Metadata

items.title: Mo Ibrahim Governance Index metadata
items.text: Metadata for a selection of indicators from the Mo Ibrahim Index of African Governance. 2023. Source: https://iiag.online/data.html

items.title: Absence of Armed Conflict
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-sec-absarmedconf Definition Measures the number of violent events that happen as a result of battles or explosions/remote violence between state forces and non-state actors. Battles are violent interactions between two politically organised armed groups and can occur between armed and organised state, non-state and external groups and in combination therein. Explosions/Remote violence are one-sided violent events in which the tool for engaging in conflict creates asymmetry by taking away the ability of the target to respond. State forces are defined as collective actors that are recognised to perform government functions, including military and police, over a given territory. Non-state actors include rebels, political and communal/identity militias and external forces. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - AbsArmedConf. Original data source/s - ACLED/UCDP

items.title: Absence of Violence against Civilians
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-sec-absviolciv Definition Measures the number of violent events against civilians perpetrated by government forces and the levels of political violence in a country. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - AbsViolCiv. Original data source/s - ACLED/PTS

items.title: Absence of Forced Migration
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-sec-absforcedmig Definition Estimates the total stock of internally displaced persons (IDPs) due to conflict and violence in a given year, calculated by MIF per 100,000 population using the World Population Prospects by UNDESA. IDMC defines IDPs as “persons or groups of persons who have been forced or obliged to flee or to leave their homes or places of habitual residence, in particular as a result of or in order to avoid the effects of armed conflict, situations of generalised violence, violations of human rights or natural or human-made disasters, and who have not crossed an internationally recognised state border.” Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - AbsForcedMig. Original data source/s - IDMC/UNHCR

items.title: Absence of Human Trafficking \& Forced Labour
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-sec-abhumtraforclab Definition Assesses the extent of human trafficking activity. Human trafficking includes activity, means and purpose, and reflects all stages of the illicit activity, from recruitment and transfer, to harbouring and receipt of persons and it does not require the movement of persons. In line with the Protocol to Prevent, Suppress and Punish Trafficking in Persons, Especially Women and Children, supplementing the United Nations Convention against Transnational Organized Crime (UNTOC), exploitation shall include, at a minimum, the exploitation or the prostitution of others or other forms of sexual exploitation, forced labour or services, slavery or practices similar to slavery, servitude or the removal of organs. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - AbHumTraForcLab. Original data source/s - ENACT/V-DEM

items.title: Absence of Crime
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-sec-abscrim Definition Assesses the extent of organised crime in a country based on the presence of criminal actors and criminal markets. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - AbsCrim. Original data source/s - ENACT/WHO

items.title: Public Perception of Security \& Safety
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-sec-pubpercsaf Definition Assesses the extent to which citizens feel safe walking in their neighbourhood. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - PubPercSaf. Original data source/s - AFR

items.title: Executive Compliance with the Rule of Law
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-law-execcomprol Definition Assesses the extent to which members of the executive (the head of state, the head of government and cabinet ministers) respect the constitution. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - ExecCompRoL. Original data source/s - V-DEM/WJP

items.title: Impartiality of the Judicial System
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-law-judsys Definition Assesses the extent to which the high court, lower courts and judges are independent. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - JudSys. Original data source/s - AFIDEP/V-DEM

items.title: Judicial Processes
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-law-judproc Definition Assesses the extent to which justice is accessible and affordable. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - JudProc. Original data source/s - V-DEM/WJP

items.title: Equality before the Law
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-law-eqtreatlaw Definition Assesses the extent to which laws, policies and practices guarantee equal treatments to various distinct groups, including ethnic, racial, religious, gender, LGBT+ and other relevant groups and the extent to which these groups are able to exercise their human rights with full equality before the law. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - EqTreatLaw. Original data source/s - FH/WJP

items.title: Law Enforcement
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-law-lawenf Definition Assesses the extent to which the police are reliable and there is a functioning system for investigating and sanctioning police misconduct. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - LawEnf. Original data source/s - AFIDEP/WEF/WJP

items.title: Property Rights
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-law-proprights Definition Assesses the extent to which government authorities ensure well-defined rights of private property and regulate the acquisition, benefits, use and sale of property. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - PropRights. Original data source/s - BS/V-DEM/WJP

items.title: Public Perception of the Rule of Law
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-law-pubpercrol Definition Assesses the extent to which citizens feel the head of the executive does not ignore the courts and laws. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - PubPercROL. Original data source/s - AFR

items.title: Institutional Checks \& Balances
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-account-instchecbal Definition Assesses the extent to which there is a working separation of powers (checks and balances). It Assesses the extent to which there is a clear separation of powers with mutual checks and balances and the basic configuration and operation of the separation of powers. It includes the subjection of state power to the law. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - InstChecBal. Original data source/s - BS/V-DEM/WJP

items.title: Absence of Undue Influence
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-account-absuninfgov Definition Assesses the extent to which democratically elected political representatives have the effective power to govern, or the extent to which there are veto powers and political enclaves. It Assesses the extent to which individuals or groups have the power to undermine democratic procedures without questioning the system as such. Veto powers can come from the military, the clergy, landowners, business elites, and external actors, among others. They can 'veto' the results of democratic decisions or retain prerogatives that cannot be touched by democratically elected officeholders (e.g., nominating the commander-in-chief of the armed forces). Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - AbsUnInfGov. Original data source/s - AFIDEP/BS/FH

items.title: Civic Checks \& Balances
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-account-civcheckbal Definition Assesses the extent to which there are effective mechanisms for citizens to make complaints and there is a right to petition and civic engagement. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - CivCheckBal. Original data source/s - BS/V-DEM/WJP

items.title: Disclosure of Public Records
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-account-discpubrec Definition Assesses the amount and timeliness of budget information that governments make publicly available in eight key budget documents in accordance with international good practice standards. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - DiscPubRec. Original data source/s - AFIDEP/IBP/WJP

items.title: Accessibility of Public Records
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-account-accpubrec Definition Assesses the extent to which publicly available records, legislative information and records of state-owned companies are accessible. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - AccPubRec. Original data source/s - AFIDEP/UNDESA/WJP

items.title: Public Perception of Accountability
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-account-pubpercacc Definition Assesses the extent to which citizens feel the head of the executive does not ignore parliament and does not just do what they want. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - PubPercAcc. Original data source/s - AFR

items.title: Anti-Corruption Mechanisms
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-corrupt-anticorrmech Definition Assesses the extent to which the government successfully contains corruption. It Assesses the extent to which adequate institutional arrangements exist to implement an anti-corruption policy and if they successfully contribute to an effective prosecution of corruption. It also Assesses the extent to which the government implements and monitors the following integrity mechanisms: auditing of state spending, regulation of party financing, citizen and media access to information, accountability of officeholders including asset declarations, conflict of interest rules, codes of conduct and transparent public procurement system. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - AntiCorrMech. Original data source/s - AFIDEP/BS

items.title: Absence of Corruption in State Institutions
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-corrupt-abscorrstateinst Definition Assesses the extent to which the judicial system is free from bribes and officials in the judicial branch do not use their public office for private gain. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - AbsCorrStateInst. Original data source/s - V-DEM/WJP

items.title: Absence of Corruption in the Public Sector
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-corrupt-abscorrpubsect Definition Assesses the extent to which public funds are not illegally being diverted and public sector employees do not engage in embezzlement and theft. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - AbsCorrPubSect. Original data source/s - V-DEM/WEF/WJP

items.title: Public Procurement Procedures
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-corrupt-pubprocuproc Definition Assesses the extent to which bids from competing contractors, suppliers, or vendors are invited through open advertising of the scope, specifications, and terms of the proposed contract, and the criteria by which the bids are evaluated are available for scrutiny. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - PubProcuProc. Original data source/s - AFIDEP/WJP

items.title: Absence of Corruption in the Private Sector
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-corrupt-abscorrprivsect Definition Assesses how common it is for firms to make undocumented extra payments or bribes connected with (a) imports and exports; (b) public utilities; © annual tax payments; (d) awarding of public contracts and licenses; (e) obtaining favourable judicial decisions. is an aggregate of these five underlying variables and the calculation is done by MIF. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - AbsCorrPrivSect. Original data source/s - WB/WEF

items.title: Public Perception of Anti-Corruption
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-corrupt-pubperccorr Definition Assesses the extent to which citizens feel the head of the executive, members of parliament and the police are not involved in corruption. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - PubPercCorr. Original data source/s - AFR

items.title: Freedom of Association \& Assembly
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-participate-freeassocass Definition Assesses the extent to which citizens are able to associate freely with their peers in public, they are free to express dissent in public settings and are free to participate in civic and political organisations. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - FreeAssocAss. Original data source/s - AFIDEP/FH

items.title: Political Pluralism
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-participate-polplur Definition Assesses the extent to which any political parties are banned, barriers to party formation exist and the opposition parties are independent from the ruling regime. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - PolPlur. Original data source/s - AFIDEP/V-DEM

items.title: Civil Society Space
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-participate-civsocspace Definition Assesses the extent to which civil society and non-governmental organisations (NGOs) can establish and operate freely. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - CivSocSpace. Original data source/s - AFIDEP/V-DEM

items.title: Democratic Elections
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-participate-demelec Definition Assesses the extent to which elections are free and fair and held regularly without irregularities and/or violence. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - DemElec. Original data source/s - AFIDEP/CDD-Ghana/V-DEM

items.title: Deliberative \& Participatory Governance
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-participate-delibpartgov Definition Measures to what extent political elites give public and reasoned justifications for their positions when important policy changes are being considered, i.e. before a decision has been made. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - DelibPartGov. Original data source/s - V-DEM

items.title: Public Perception of Political Participation
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-participate-pubpercelecfree Definition Assesses the extent to which citizens feel the last national election was free and fair as well as the extent to which citizens trust the national electoral commission. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - PubPercElecFree. Original data source/s - AFR

items.title: Personal Liberties
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-rights-perslibert Definition Assesses the extent to which there is freedom of foreign travel and emigration and the extent to which citizens enjoy freedom of movement and residence without restrictions by public authorities. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - PersLibert. Original data source/s - FH/V-DEM/WJP

items.title: Freedom of Expression \& Belief
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-rights-freeexpbelief Definition Assesses the extent to which citizens enjoy freedom to express personal opinion and academic freedom. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - FreeExpBelief. Original data source/s - FH/V-DEM/WJP

items.title: Media Freedom
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-rights-mediafree Definition Assesses the extent to which the political context allows journalists to do their work freely as well as the extent to which media is impartial. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - MediaFree. Original data source/s - AFIDEP/RSF/V-DEM

items.title: Digital Freedom
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-rights-digrights Definition Assesses the extent to which there is a legal framework to protect internet users' privacy and their data. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - DigRights. Original data source/s - AFIDEP/DSP\&V-DEM/V-DEM

items.title: Protection against Discrimination
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-rights-protagdiscrim Definition Assesses the extent to which there is an official mechanism that receives and investigates complaints of ethnic discrimination (whether investigations are undertaken independently or in collaboration with other entities). It also Assesses the extent to which, in the last year, the mechanism has been proactive and implemented programs designed to reduce/combat ethnic discrimination (such as conducting sensitisation campaigns), and the mechanism is accessible to most members of minority ethnic groups. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - ProtAgDiscrim. Original data source/s - AFIDEP

items.title: Public Perception of Freedom of Speech
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-rights-pubfreespafr Definition Assesses the extent to which citizens feel free to say what they think. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - PubFreeSpAFR. Original data source/s - AFR

items.title: Equal Political Power
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-inclusion-eqpolpow Definition Assesses the extent to which political power is distributed according to socioeconomic position. is concerned not with the degree of social inequality but rather with the political eﬀects of this inequality. Speciﬁcally, the extent to which wealth and income translates into political power. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - EqPolPow. Original data source/s - V-DEM

items.title: Equal Political Representation
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-inclusion-eqpolrep Definition Assesses to the extent to which various segments of the population (including ethnic, racial, religious, gender, LGBT+, and other relevant groups) have full political rights and electoral opportunities. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - EqPolRep. Original data source/s - FH/IPU/V-DEM

items.title: Equal Civil Liberties
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-inclusion-equcivlib Definition Estimates the percentage of the total population of the country that lives in the areas where government oﬃcials’ respect for civil liberties is signiﬁcantly weaker than the country average. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - EquCivLib. Original data source/s - V-DEM

items.title: Equal Socioeconomic Opportunity
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-inclusion-eqsoceconopp Definition Assesses the extent to which state jobs and state business opportunities are equally open to qualified individuals regardless of socioeconomic position. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - EqSocEconOpp. Original data source/s - AFIDEP/V-DEM

items.title: Equal Access to Public Services
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-inclusion-eqaccpubserv Definition Assesses the extent to which access to basic public services, such as order and security, primary education, clean water, and healthcare, are distributed equally according to socioeconomic position. Assesses the extent to which the socio-economic position is an important cleavage in society for the distribution of public services. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - EqAccPubServ. Original data source/s - V-DEM

items.title: Public Perception of Inclusion \& Equality
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-inclusion-pubpercinceq Definition Assesses the extent to which citizens feel their ethnic group is treated fairly by the government. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - PubPercIncEq. Original data source/s - AFR

items.title: Political Power \& Representation of Women
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-women-polpowrepwom Definition Assesses the extent to which political power is distributed according to gender. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - PolPowRepWom. Original data source/s - AFIDEP/IPU/V-DEM

items.title: Rights \& Civil Liberties for Women
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-women-eqcivlibwom Definition Assesses the extent to which women enjoy the same level of civil liberties as men. Here, civil liberties are understood to include access to justice, private property rights, freedom of movement, and freedom from forced labour. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - EqCivLibWom. Original data source/s - V-DEM/WB

items.title: Socioeconomic Opportunity for Women
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-women-socioeconoppwom Definition Assesses the extent to which state jobs and state business opportunities are equally open to qualified individuals or firms regardless of gender. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - SocioEconOppWom. Original data source/s - AFIDEP/V-DEM

items.title: Access to Public Services for Women
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-women-accpubservwomvdem Definition Assesses the extent to which access to basic public services, such as order and security, primary education, clean water, and healthcare, are distributed equally according to gender. Assesses if gender is an important cleavage in society for the distribution of public services. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - AccPubServWomVDEM. Original data source/s - V-DEM

items.title: Laws on Violence against Women
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-women-lawsviolencewomwb Definition Assesses the extent to which there are laws and regulations on violence against women by assessing whether there is legislation specifically addressing domestic violence, there is legislation on sexual harassment in employment, there are criminal penalties or civil remedies for sexual harassment in employment, whether the law is free of legal provisions that require a married woman to obey her husband, and whether a woman can obtain a judgment of divorce in the same way as a man. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - LawsViolenceWomWB. Original data source/s - WB

items.title: Public Perception of Female Leadership
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-women-pubpercfemleadafr Definition This indicator Assesses the extent to which citizens believe women should have the same chance of being elected to political office as men. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - PubPercFemLeadAFR. Original data source/s - AFR

items.title: Civil Registration
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-pubadmin-civreg Definition Assesses to the extent to which citizens can obtain birth certification upon request and both of the following conditions are met: certification is provided within 30 days or less; and certification is provided for no charge. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - CivREg. Original data source/s - AFIDEP

items.title: Capacity of the Statistical System
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-pubadmin-capstatsys Definition Assesses the capacity of a country's national statistical system based on a diagnostic framework thereby assessing three dimensions: methodology, source data, periodicity and timeliness. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - CapStatSys. Original data source/s - AFIDEP/ODW/V-DEM/WB

items.title: Effective Administration
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-pubadmin-effadmin Definition Assesses the extent to which appointments to the civil service are merit-based and the extent to which public officials perform their duties rigorously and impartially. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - EffAdmin. Original data source/s - AfDB/AFIDEP/V-DEM/WB

items.title: Tax \& Revenue Mobilisation
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-pubadmin-taxrevmob Definition Measures total non-resource tax revenue, excluding social contributions. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - TaxRevMob. Original data source/s - AfDB/UNU-WIDER/WB

items.title: Budgetary \& Financial Management
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-pubadmin-budgmgmt Definition Assesses the extent to which there is: (i) a comprehensive and credible budget, linked to policy priorities, which in turn are linked to a poverty reduction strategy, (ii) effective financial management systems to ensure that incurred expenditures are consistent with the approved budget, that budgeted revenues are achieved, and that aggregate fiscal control is maintained, (iii) timely and accurate fiscal reporting, including timely and audited public accounts and effective arrangements for follow up and (iv) clear and balanced assignment of expenditures and revenues to each level of government. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - BudgMgmt. Original data source/s - AfDB/WB

items.title: Public Perception of Easiness to Obtain an Identity Document
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-pubadmin-pubpercpubadmin Definition Assesses citizens' perceptions of how easy it is to obtain the identity documents they needed. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - PubPercPubAdmin. Original data source/s - AFR

items.title: Regional Integration
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-business-reginteg Definition Assesses the extent to which government’s actions and efforts (i) promote free movement of persons and labour and easy right of establishment; and (ii) contribute to regional financial integration. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - RegInteg. Original data source/s - AfDB/AfDB\&AUC/UNCTAD

items.title: Economic Diversification
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-business-econdiv Definition Assesses to which degree exports and imports of a country are concentrated on a few products rather than being distributed in a more homogenous manner among several products. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - EconDiv. Original data source/s - OEC/UNCTAD

items.title: Business \& Competition Regulations
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-business-buscompreg Definition Assesses the extent to which the legal, regulatory, and policy environments help or hinder private businesses in investing, creating jobs and becoming more productive. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - BusCompReg. Original data source/s - AfDB/BS/WB

items.title: Access to Banking Services
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-business-accbankserv Definition Measures bank branches and ATMs per 100,000 adults. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - AccBankServ. Original data source/s - IMF/WB

items.title: Labour Relations
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-business-labrel Definition Assesses the extent to which labour-employer relations are generally cooperative. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - LabRel. Original data source/s - AFIDEP/ITUC/WEF

items.title: Secure Employment Opportunities
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-business-secemplopp Definition Measures the percentage of employed persons living in poverty in spite of being employed. Poverty is defined using the international poverty line of US$1.90 per day in purchasing power parity (PPP). Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - SecEmplOpp. Original data source/s - ILO/WB

items.title: Public Perception of Economic Opportunities
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-business-sateo Definition Assesses extent to which citizens feel the government is doing well at creating jobs. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - SatEO. Original data source/s - AFR

items.title: Transport Network
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-infrastructure-transpnet Definition Measures the extent of the total road network in km (per km2 of exploitable land area) and the total paved roads (km per 10,000 inhabitants) in a country. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - TranspNet. Original data source/s - AfDB/WEF

items.title: Access to Energy
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-infrastructure-accenergy Definition Measures the percentage of the population with access to electricity. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - AccEnergy. Original data source/s - WB/WHO

items.title: Mobile Communications
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-infrastructure-mobcomm Definition Measures the number of subscriptions to a public mobile-telephone service that provide access to the public switched telephone network (PSTN) using cellular technology. It includes the number of postpaid subscriptions and the number of active prepaid accounts, divided by population and multiplied by 100. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - MobComm. Original data source/s - ITU

items.title: Internet \& Computers
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-infrastructure-digacc Definition Measures the proportion of households that have a computer. A computer refers to a desktop computer, a laptop (portable) computer or a tablet (or similar handheld computer). Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - DigAcc. Original data source/s - ITU/Cable/WB

items.title: Shipping \& Postal Network
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-infrastructure-shippostfac Definition Assesses the extent of a country's integration level into global shipping liner networks, using six components: i) the number of scheduled ships calls per week in the country; ii) deployed annual capacity in Twenty-Foot-equivalent Units (TEU): total deployed capacity offered at the country; iii) the number of regular liner shipping services from and to the country; iv) the number of liner shipping companies that provide services from and to the country; v) the average size in TEU of the ships deployed by the scheduled service with the largest average vessel size; vi) the number of the other countries that are connected to the country through direct liner shipping services. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - ShipPostFac. Original data source/s - UNCTAD/UPU

items.title: Public Perception of Infrastructure
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-infrastructure-satinfr Definition Assesses the extent to which citizens feel the government is doing well at maintaining roads and bridges. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - SatInfr. Original data source/s - AFR

items.title: Rural Land \& Water Access
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-rural-rurlandwatacc Definition Assesses the extent to which access to land is afforded to rural people via the policy framework, which is key to their capacity to undergo rural transformation. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - RurLandWatAcc. Original data source/s - IFAD

items.title: Rural Market Access
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-rural-rurmarkaccifad Definition Assesses the extent to which existing agricultural input markets and produce markets are reliably providing value for money to smallholders for inputs and the highest proportion of the retail price for produce. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - RurMarkAccIFAD. Original data source/s - IFAD

items.title: Rural Economy Support
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-rural-rurecosupp Definition Assesses the extent to which government prioritises strategies for and investment in the rural poor and is actively promoting the development of a robust private sector in rural areas. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - RurEcoSupp. Original data source/s - IFAD

items.title: Rural Representation \& Participation
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-rural-rurreppart Definition Assesses the extent to which rural people can organize into autonomous groups. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - RurRepPart. Original data source/s - IFAD

items.title: Access to Healthcare
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-health-acchealth Definition Estimates how much households in each country are spending on health directly out of pocket. It Estimates the share of out of pocket payment of total current health expenditures. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - AccHealth. Original data source/s - V-DEM/WHO

items.title: Access to Water \& Sanitation
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-health-accwatersanit Definition Measures the availability of drinking water from an improved source, provided collection time is not more than 30 minutes for a round trip, including queuing. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - AccWaterSanit. Original data source/s - WHO\&UNICEF

items.title: Control of Communicable Diseases
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-health-contcomdis Definition Measures the estimated number of deaths from malaria in a given year, calculated by MIF per 100,000 of the population using the World Population Prospects by UNDESA. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - ContComDis. Original data source/s - UNAIDS/WHO

items.title: Control of Non-Communicable Diseases
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-health-contnoncomdis Definition Measures the age-standardised percentage of DALY's (disability adjusted life years) due to metabolic risks. Disability-Adjusted Life Years (DALYs) are defined as the sum of years lost due to premature death and years lived with disability. DALYs are also defined as years of healthy life lost. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - ContNonComDis. Original data source/s - IHME

items.title: Child \& Maternal Health
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-health-contchildmathealth Definition Measures the probability of dying between birth and exact age 5, expressed per 1,000 live births. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - ContChildMatHealth. Original data source/s - UN IGME/UN MMEIG/UNDESA/UNICEF\&WHO\&WB

items.title: Health Quality
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-health-healthqual Definition Measures compliance with 1 out of 15 WHO International Health Regulations core capacity scores: Human resources. The revised International Health Regulations (IHR) were adopted in 2005 and entered into force in 2007. Under the IHR, States Parties are obliged to develop and maintain minimum core capacities for surveillance and response, including at points of entry, in order to early detect, assess, notify, and respond to any potential public health events of international concern. The 15 capacities are: C1. Policy, legal and normative instruments to implement IHR, C2. IHR coordination and National IHR Focal Point, C.3. Financing, C4. Laboratory, C5. Surveillance, C6. Human resources, C7. Health emergency management, C8. Health services provision, C9. Infection prevention and control (IPC) C10. Risk communication and community engagement (RCCE), C11. Points of entry (PoEs) and border health, C12. Zoonotic diseases, C13. Food safety, C14. Chemical events, C15. Radiation emergencies. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - HealthQual. Original data source/s - WHO

items.title: Public Perception of Health Provision
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-health-sathealth Definition Assesses citizens' perceptions of how easy it is to obtain the medical care they need. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - SatHealth. Original data source/s - AFR

items.title: Equality in Education
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-education-equeduc Definition Measures the ratio of female gross enrolment to male gross enrolment in primary and lower secondary education. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - EquEduc. Original data source/s - V-DEM/UNESCO

items.title: Education Enrolment
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-education-educenr Definition Measures the number of students enrolled in pre-primary level of education, regardless of age, expressed as a percentage of the eligible official school-age population corresponding to pre-primary education. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - EducEnr. Original data source/s - UNESCO

items.title: Education Completion
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-education-compeduc Definition Measures the percentage of a cohort of children or young people aged 3-5 years above the intended age for the last grade of the primary level of education who have completed that grade. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - CompEduc. Original data source/s - UNESCO

items.title: Human Resources in Education
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-education-educres Definition Measures the average number of pupils per qualified teacher at the primary level of education in a given academic year. A qualified teacher is one who has at least the minimum academic qualifications required for teaching their subjects at the relevant level in a given country in a given academic year. The higher the pupil/qualified teacher ratio, the lower the relative access of pupils to qualified teachers. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - EducRes. Original data source/s - UNESCO

items.title: Education Quality
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-education-eduqqual Definition Assesses the extent to which there are solid institutions for basic, secondary, and tertiary education, as well as for research and development. It Assesses whether education policy is successful in delivering high quality education and training, and research and development receive effective support from the government. It does not focus on expenditures alone, but also on the quality and competitiveness of the education system and the research sector, considering the structure of funding and knowledge providers (public, private and international cooperation), the output of the educational and developmental efforts, e.g. enrolment rates, literacy rates, percentage of people with higher education and number of patent applications. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - EduqQual. Original data source/s - BS/WB/WEF

items.title: Public Perception of Education Provision
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-education-sateduc Definition Assesses citizens' perceptions of how easy it was to obtain the services they needed from teachers or school officials. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - SatEduc. Original data source/s - AFR

items.title: Social Safety Nets
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-social-socialsafnet Definition Assesses the extent to which there are departments or equivalent mandated to ensure social safety nets for the elderly, those with an illness or health conditions and the unemployed. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - SocialSafNet. Original data source/s - AFIDEP/BS

items.title: Poverty Reduction Policies
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-social-povredpol Definition Assesses the extent to which significant parts of the population are fundamentally excluded from society due to poverty and inequality. It Assesses the level of social exclusion as a determining factor for economic transformation. It refers to structural exclusion and not to the output strength of a country’s economy as reflected in macroeconomic data like unemployment or GDP growth. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - PovRedPol. Original data source/s - AfDB/BS/WB

items.title: Socioeconomic Inequality Mitigation
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-social-ineqmitig Definition Assesses the extent to which the pattern of public expenditures and revenue collection affects the poor and is consistent with national poverty reduction priorities. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - IneqMitig. Original data source/s - AfDB/FFP/WB/WID.World

items.title: Decent Housing
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-social-dechous Definition Measures the proportion of the urban population living in slums. Slum households are households whose members suffer one or more of the following ‘household deprivations’: i) Lack of access to improved water source, ii) Lack of access to improved sanitation facilities, iii) Lack of sufficient living area, iv)Lack of housing durability and, v) Lack of security of tenure. Estimations do not include security of tenure due to data limitations. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - DecHous. Original data source/s - CAHF/UN-Habitat

items.title: Food Security
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-social-foodsec Definition Measures the prevalence of undernourishment, expressed as the probability that a randomly selected individual from the population consumes an amount of calories that is insufficient to cover her/his energy requirement for an active and healthy life. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - FoodSec. Original data source/s - FAO

items.title: Public Perception of Social Protection \& Lived Poverty
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-social-livpovpubpercsocprot Definition Assesses citizens' perceptions of their present living conditions. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - LivPovPubPercSocProt. Original data source/s - AFR

items.title: Promotion of Environmental Sustainability
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-environ-promenvsust Definition Assesses the extent to which environmental concerns are effectively taken into account in both macro and microeconomic terms. It considers the extent to which externalisation of costs or inadequate time horizons are avoided or restrained by environmental regulation. In macroeconomic terms, it Assesses the extent to which tax and energy policies take ecological goals and Measures into account (e.g. promotion of renewable energies, CO2 reduction goals). In microeconomic terms, it Assesses the extent to which the government sets incentives for environmentally sound consumption and investments to households and companies. A deeply ingrained awareness of the environment or nature in society may serve as a functional equivalent. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - PromEnvSust. Original data source/s - AfDB/BS/WB

items.title: Enforcement of Environmental Policies
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-environ-enfenvpol Definition Assesses the extent to which environmental protection authorities enforce environmental laws. This is based on the following criteria: the extent to which companies comply with the law after notification of violations from environmental protection authorities; firms get audited or inspected by environmental protection authorities as a result of allegations of pollution by neighbours; firms are routinely audited by the environmental protection authorities; it is likely that environmental protection authorities will impose sanctions if violations are detected. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - EnfEnvPol. Original data source/s - WEF/WJP

items.title: Air Quality
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-environ-airqual Definition Measures the ratio of disability-adjusted life-years (DALYs) to the population per 100,000 people due to air pollution. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - AirQual. Original data source/s - HEI\&IHME

items.title: Sustainable Use of Land \& Forests
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-environ-sususelandfor Definition Measures the diversification of agricultural production as well as the extent to which the agricultural area is under certified organic agriculture. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - SusUseLandFor. Original data source/s - FAO/WB/WRI

items.title: Land \& Water Biodiversity Protection
items.text: Field Value Variable Id iiag-environ-landwatbiodiv Definition Assesses a country’s efforts to achieve 30% protection for all biomes within its borders. It is calculated using the percentage of each country’s biomes covered by protected areas. It is calculated using the proportions of the area of each of a country’s biome types that are covered by protected areas and then constructing a weighted sum of the protection percentages for all biomes within that country. Protection percentages are weighted according to the prevalence of each biome type within that country. Format 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-49%; 3=50-69%; 4=70-89%; 5=>89% Other Metadata IIAG Series Id - LandWatBiodiv. Original data source/s - ENACT/WB/Yale\&Columbia

items.title: ICT infrastructure metadata
items.text: Metadata employed in data collection using Perplexity Deep Research.

items.title: Electricity Access Rate
items.text: Field Value Variable ict-energy-elecaccess Definition Percentage of national population with access to electricity (grid or off-grid). This is the primary indicator of energy infrastructure availability for ICT. Based on World Bank/IEA Tracking SDG7 data, national statistical offices, or Afrobarometer surveys. Format Categorical: 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<25% population; 2=25-49%; 3=50-74%; 4=75-89%; 5=90%+ Other Metadata Primary sources: World Bank WDI (SE.ELC.ACCS.ZS), IEA/IRENA/UNSD/World Bank SDG7 Tracking, Afrobarometer. Available for all 54 African countries. Updated annually. Data Collection Guidelines Search: World Bank data portal 'Access to electricity (% of population)', IEA SDG7 tracking database, '[Country] electricity access rate', national energy statistics. Use most recent year available (2022-2025 preferred).

items.title: Urban-Rural Electrification Gap
items.text: Field Value Variable ict-energy-urbanruraldevide Definition The gap between urban and rural electricity access rates. A smaller gap indicates more equitable energy distribution, critical for ensuring ICT infrastructure can serve the whole population, not just cities. Format Categorical: 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=Gap >60 percentage points; 2=Gap 41-60pp; 3=Gap 21-40pp; 4=Gap 10-20pp; 5=Gap <10pp Other Metadata Primary sources: World Bank WDI (urban/rural electrification rates), IEA, Afrobarometer. Urban and rural rates are separately tracked by most international databases. Difference calculated as urban rate minus rural rate. Data Collection Guidelines Search: World Bank 'Access to electricity, urban (% of urban population)' and 'Access to electricity, rural (% of rural population)' for [Country]. Calculate the gap. Afrobarometer Round 9/10 also provides urban vs rural energy access data.

items.title: Grid Reliability (Power Outage Frequency)
items.text: Field Value Variable ict-energy-reliability Definition Frequency and severity of power outages experienced by businesses and households. Based on World Bank Enterprise Surveys, Afrobarometer, or national utility data. Reflects the quality of electricity supply beyond mere access — critical for running ICT equipment and data infrastructure. Format Categorical: 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=Severe (frequent daily outages or regular load shedding); 2=Poor (weekly outages, intermittent load shedding); 3=Moderate (monthly outages, occasional disruptions); 4=Good (rare outages, <10 per year); 5=Excellent (near-continuous supply, <5 outages per year) Other Metadata Primary sources: World Bank Enterprise Surveys (infrastructure module - power outages per month), Afrobarometer energy module, national utility reliability reports, SAIDI/SAIFI indices where available. Enterprise Surveys cover most African countries. Data Collection Guidelines Search: World Bank Enterprise Surveys '[Country]' infrastructure indicators, Afrobarometer '[Country] electricity reliability', '[Country] power outage frequency', national utility SAIDI/SAIFI data, '[Country] load shedding schedule'. News reports on grid stability.

items.title: Renewable Energy Share of Generation
items.text: Field Value Variable ict-energy-renewableshare Definition Percentage of total electricity generation from renewable sources (hydro, solar, wind, geothermal, biomass). Indicates energy sustainability and potential for distributed power solutions relevant to ICT infrastructure in off-grid areas. Format Categorical: 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<10%; 2=10-24%; 3=25-49%; 4=50-74%; 5=75%+ Other Metadata Primary sources: IRENA Renewable Energy Statistics, IEA electricity generation data, World Bank WDI (EG.ELC.RNEW.ZS), national energy ministry reports. Available for virtually all African countries via IRENA. Data Collection Guidelines Search: IRENA Country Profiles '[Country]', World Bank 'Renewable electricity output (% of total)' for [Country], IEA '[Country] electricity generation by source', national energy ministry annual reports.

items.title: Electricity Affordability
items.text: Field Value Variable ict-energy-affordability Definition Affordability of electricity for residential and small business consumers, measured as cost of electricity relative to income levels. High electricity costs are a direct barrier to ICT adoption and data center viability. Format Categorical: 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=Very Expensive (>10% of household income for basic service); 2=Expensive (6-10%); 3=Moderate (3-5%); 4=Affordable (1-2%); 5=Very Affordable (<1%) Other Metadata Primary sources: World Bank Enterprise Surveys (electricity costs), national energy regulatory authority tariff schedules, ESMAP reports, Doing Business/B-READY indicators, Alliance for Affordable Internet (cost benchmarks). Residential tariff per kWh can be compared to GNI per capita. Data Collection Guidelines Search: '[Country] electricity tariff residential', national energy regulatory authority tariff schedules, World Bank Enterprise Surveys electricity cost data, ESMAP energy affordability reports, '[Country] cost of electricity per kWh USD'. Compare to GNI per capita.

items.title: Off-Grid and Distributed Energy Policy
items.text: Field Value Variable ict-energy-offgridpolicy Definition Whether the country has established policies, regulations, and programmes supporting off-grid and distributed energy solutions (mini-grids, solar home systems). These are critical for extending ICT infrastructure to unserved areas beyond the central grid. Format Categorical: 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=No policy framework; 2=Basic policy exists but limited implementation; 3=Active policy with some deployment; 4=Comprehensive framework with significant deployment; 5=Mature ecosystem with widespread deployment Other Metadata Primary sources: RISE (Regulatory Indicators for Sustainable Energy) - World Bank/ESMAP, GOGLA market reports, SE4All country action agendas, national rural electrification strategies. RISE covers most African countries. Data Collection Guidelines Search: RISE database (rise.esmap.org) '[Country]' mini-grid and off-grid indicators, GOGLA '[Country] off-grid solar market', SE4All '[Country]', national rural electrification agency, Power Africa '[Country]', '[Country] mini-grid regulation'.

items.title: Mobile Phone Penetration
items.text: Field Value Variable ict-connectivity-mobilepen Definition Unique mobile subscriber penetration rate as a percentage of the total population. Measures the proportion of individuals who subscribe to a mobile service (not SIM cards, which can be multiple per person). Mobile phones are the primary device for internet access in Africa. Format Categorical: 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<25%; 2=25-39%; 3=40-54%; 4=55-69%; 5=70%+ Other Metadata Primary sources: GSMA Intelligence (unique subscribers), ITU World Telecommunication Indicators, national telecom regulator reports. GSMA data available for all African countries. Unique subscriber penetration more meaningful than SIM penetration. Data Collection Guidelines Search: GSMA Intelligence '[Country] unique subscribers', GSMA Mobile Connectivity Index data download, ITU '[Country] mobile subscriptions', national telecom regulator annual reports, DataReportal '[Country] digital report'.

items.title: Smartphone Adoption
items.text: Field Value Variable ict-connectivity-smartphonepen Definition Percentage of mobile connections using smartphones (as opposed to feature phones or basic phones). Smartphones are the minimum device requirement for accessing most digital public infrastructure services, apps, and mobile internet. Format Categorical: 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<20%; 2=20-39%; 3=40-59%; 4=60-79%; 5=80%+ Other Metadata Primary sources: GSMA Intelligence (smartphone adoption), Pew Research Center Global Attitudes surveys, national ICT household surveys. GSMA provides annual estimates for all African countries. Data Collection Guidelines Search: GSMA Intelligence '[Country] smartphone adoption', GSMA Mobile Economy Sub-Saharan Africa report, Pew Research '[Country] smartphone', DataReportal '[Country]', national ICT surveys.

items.title: 4G/LTE Population Coverage
items.text: Field Value Variable ict-connectivity-4gcoverage Definition Percentage of the national population covered by at least one 4G/LTE mobile network signal, whether or not they subscribe to the service. 4G is the practical minimum for meaningful broadband connectivity enabling digital services. Format Categorical: 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<25%; 2=25-49%; 3=50-74%; 4=75-89%; 5=90%+ Other Metadata Primary sources: GSMA Intelligence, ITU World Telecommunication/ICT Indicators Database, national telecom regulators, OpenSignal. Data available for all African countries via ITU and GSMA. Data Collection Guidelines Search: ITU '[Country] 4G coverage population', GSMA Mobile Connectivity Index '[Country]', national telecom regulator annual report, OpenSignal '[Country]', GSMA Mobile Economy Sub-Saharan Africa.

items.title: Internet Usage Rate
items.text: Field Value Variable ict-connectivity-internetuse Definition Percentage of individuals who used the internet (from any location, via any device) in the last three months. This measures actual internet adoption beyond network availability, capturing the combined effect of infrastructure, affordability, digital literacy, and relevance. Format Categorical: 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<15%; 2=15-29%; 3=30-49%; 4=50-69%; 5=70%+ Other Metadata Primary sources: ITU World Telecommunication Indicators (individuals using the internet %), World Bank WDI (IT.NET.USER.ZS), Afrobarometer, DataReportal. Available for all African countries. ITU provides annual estimates. Data Collection Guidelines Search: ITU '[Country] individuals using the internet', World Bank 'Individuals using the Internet (% of population)' for [Country], Afrobarometer internet use data, DataReportal '[Country] digital report'.

items.title: Mobile Data Affordability
items.text: Field Value Variable ict-connectivity-dataafford Definition Affordability of mobile broadband data, measured as the cost of a basic mobile data plan (1-2GB) as a percentage of GNI per capita. The UN Broadband Commission target is below 2% of monthly GNI per capita. Affordability is the single largest barrier to internet adoption in Africa. Format Categorical: 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=Very Expensive (>10% GNI p.c.); 2=Expensive (5-10%); 3=Moderate (2-5%); 4=Affordable (1-2%); 5=Meets UN target (<1%) Other Metadata Primary sources: ITU ICT Price Baskets, Alliance for Affordable Internet (A4AI), Cable.co.uk annual data pricing study, Research ICT Africa. ITU publishes affordability data for all African countries annually. Data Collection Guidelines Search: ITU ICT Price Baskets '[Country]', A4AI Affordability Report, Cable.co.uk worldwide mobile data pricing '[Country]', Research ICT Africa pricing data, '[Country] 1GB mobile data cost USD'. Compare to GNI per capita.

items.title: International Internet Bandwidth
items.text: Field Value Variable ict-connectivity-intlbandwidth Definition The country's total international internet bandwidth capacity per internet user, indicating the quality and capacity of the country's connection to the global internet. Determined by submarine cable landings (for coastal states), cross-border terrestrial fibre (for landlocked states), and satellite links. Format Categorical: 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=Very Low (<5 kbit/s per user); 2=Low (5-19 kbit/s); 3=Moderate (20-49 kbit/s); 4=Good (50-99 kbit/s); 5=Strong (100+ kbit/s per user) Other Metadata Primary sources: ITU World Telecommunication Indicators (international bandwidth per internet user), TeleGeography, national telecom regulators. ITU publishes this indicator for most African countries. Submarine cable landings data from TeleGeography Submarine Cable Map. Data Collection Guidelines Search: ITU '[Country] international internet bandwidth', TeleGeography '[Country] submarine cable', national telecom regulator statistics, '[Country] international bandwidth capacity'. For landlocked states, note cross-border fibre connectivity.

items.title: Internet Exchange Point Presence
items.text: Field Value Variable ict-connectivity-ixp Definition Whether the country has an operational Internet Exchange Point (IXP) and its maturity. IXPs enable local internet traffic to be exchanged domestically rather than being routed internationally, reducing latency, costs, and dependence on foreign infrastructure. Critical for local digital ecosystem development. Format Categorical: 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=No IXP; 2=IXP planned or under development; 3=Single IXP with limited participation; 4=Active IXP(s) with moderate participation; 5=Mature IXP ecosystem with broad participation Other Metadata Primary sources: Packet Clearing House (PCH) IXP directory, African IXP Association (Af-IX), PeeringDB, Internet Society IXP reports. PCH maintains a comprehensive global directory. Most data is publicly accessible. Data Collection Guidelines Search: Packet Clearing House IXP directory '[Country]', PeeringDB '[Country]', Af-IX member directory, Internet Society '[Country] IXP', '[Country] internet exchange point'. Note number of participants and traffic volumes if available.

items.title: Commercial Data Center Presence
items.text: Field Value Variable ict-storage-dcpresence Definition The presence and tier quality of commercial/colocation data center facilities operating in the country. Commercial data centers provide the physical infrastructure for hosting cloud services, content delivery, and enterprise applications domestically. Format Categorical: 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=No commercial data centers; 2=Small-scale facilities only (Tier I-II); 3=At least one Tier III facility; 4=Multiple Tier III facilities or at least one Tier IV; 5=Mature data center market with multiple operators and hyperscale presence Other Metadata Primary sources: Cloudscene data center directory, Data Center Map, Xalam Analytics, Africa Data Centres reports, operator websites (Teraco, Equinix, Raxio, PAIX, MainOne, Liquid). Industry is well-documented due to investment interest. Data Collection Guidelines Search: Cloudscene '[Country] data centers', Data Center Map '[Country]', '[Country] colocation data center', Xalam Analytics Africa data center report, Africa Data Centres '[Country]', Raxio '[Country]'. Note tier levels and major operators.

items.title: Government Data Center / Cloud Strategy
items.text: Field Value Variable ict-storage-govcloud Definition Whether the government has established dedicated data center infrastructure and/or a government cloud (G-Cloud) strategy for hosting e-government services and public sector data domestically. Critical for data sovereignty and e-government service delivery. Format Categorical: 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=No government data center or cloud strategy; 2=Government data center planned or under construction; 3=Basic government data center operational; 4=National data center operational with cloud strategy adopted; 5=Mature government cloud/data center ecosystem with active migration Other Metadata Primary sources: National ICT/digital transformation strategies, e-government agency websites, ministry of ICT reports, World Bank Digital Development country diagnostics, ITU national cybersecurity and ICT reports. Data Collection Guidelines Search: '[Country] national data center', '[Country] government cloud strategy', '[Country] e-government data center', ministry of ICT/digital economy reports, World Bank '[Country] digital development', UN e-Government Survey country profile.

items.title: Data Localisation Requirements
items.text: Field Value Variable ict-storage-datalocalisation Definition Whether the country has enacted legal or regulatory requirements mandating that certain categories of data (personal data, government data, financial data, or critical infrastructure data) be stored within national borders. Central to data sovereignty debates in Africa. Format Categorical: 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=No data localisation requirements; 2=Proposed or draft legislation; 3=Sectoral requirements only (e.g., financial or telecoms data); 4=Comprehensive requirements for personal data or broad categories; 5=Strict requirements covering multiple data types with enforcement Other Metadata Primary sources: UNCTAD Data Protection and Privacy Legislation tracker, Data Protection Africa portal, national data protection authority websites, DLA Piper Global Data Protection Laws database, AU Convention on Cyber Security ratification status. Data Collection Guidelines Search: UNCTAD '[Country] data protection legislation', DLA Piper '[Country] data protection', Data Protection Africa '[Country]', '[Country] data localisation law', '[Country] data residency requirements', national data protection authority website.

items.title: Data Protection Legislation
items.text: Field Value Variable ict-storage-dataprotection Definition Whether the country has enacted comprehensive data protection and privacy legislation. A foundational legal requirement for building trust in digital public infrastructure and for enabling secure cross-border data flows under frameworks like the AU Convention on Cyber Security and Personal Data Protection. Format Categorical: 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=No legislation; 2=Draft legislation or partial provisions in other laws; 3=Legislation enacted but no enforcement authority; 4=Legislation enacted with Data Protection Authority established; 5=Mature framework with active enforcement and regional alignment Other Metadata Primary sources: UNCTAD Cyberlaw Tracker (data protection section), Data Protection Africa, African Union Convention ratification tracker, national gazette/legal databases. UNCTAD covers all African countries. Data Collection Guidelines Search: UNCTAD Cyberlaw Tracker '[Country]', Data Protection Africa '[Country]', '[Country] data protection act', '[Country] data protection authority', AU Convention on Cyber Security ratification status, DLA Piper data protection laws of the world.

items.title: Cloud Services Availability
items.text: Field Value Variable ict-storage-cloudadoption Definition The availability and presence of major cloud service providers (hyperscale and regional) operating in or serving the country with local or near-local infrastructure. Cloud availability determines access to scalable computing resources for government, business, and startups. Format Categorical: 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=No local or regional cloud presence; 2=Served remotely from distant regions only (>100ms latency); 3=Regional cloud availability zone nearby (same sub-region); 4=At least one major provider with local presence or edge node; 5=Multiple hyperscale providers with local availability zones Other Metadata Primary sources: AWS, Microsoft Azure, Google Cloud, Huawei Cloud, Oracle Cloud region availability maps, local cloud provider directories. Hyperscale expansion into Africa is well-documented in trade press. Data Collection Guidelines Search: AWS Global Infrastructure map Africa, Microsoft Azure regions Africa, Google Cloud regions Africa, '[Country] cloud data center', '[Country] cloud services provider', Huawei Cloud Africa, Oracle Cloud Africa. Note nearest availability zone and latency estimates.

items.title: National Cybersecurity Readiness
items.text: Field Value Variable ict-storage-cybersecurity Definition The country's overall cybersecurity preparedness, based on the ITU Global Cybersecurity Index (GCI). Covers legal measures, technical measures, organisational measures, capacity development, and cooperation. Essential for trust in stored data and digital services. Format Categorical: 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=GCI score <20 (Building); 2=GCI score 20-39 (Evolving); 3=GCI score 40-59 (Establishing); 4=GCI score 60-79 (Advancing); 5=GCI score 80+ (Role-modelling) Other Metadata Primary sources: ITU Global Cybersecurity Index (published biennially, most recent 2024). Covers all African countries. Score is 0-100 across 5 pillars: legal, technical, organisational, capacity development, cooperation. Data Collection Guidelines Search: ITU Global Cybersecurity Index '[Country]', GCI 2024 country profile, '[Country] cybersecurity readiness score'. The ITU GCI database at itu.int/en/ITU-D/Cybersecurity/Pages/global-cybersecurity-index.aspx provides country scores.

items.title: Basic Digital Literacy
items.text: Field Value Variable ict-capacity-digitalliteracy Definition The proportion of the population with basic digital skills (ability to use a computer, smartphone, or the internet for simple tasks such as sending messages, finding information, or using apps). Based on ITU household survey data, Afrobarometer, or national ICT surveys. Format Categorical: 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=<10% of population; 2=10-24%; 3=25-39%; 4=40-59%; 5=60%+ Other Metadata Primary sources: ITU household ICT survey indicators (basic digital skills), Afrobarometer internet/ICT literacy questions, UNESCO UIS ICT in Education data, national digital economy assessments. ITU publishes estimates for most countries. Data Collection Guidelines Search: ITU '[Country] ICT skills', ITU household ICT survey data, Afrobarometer '[Country] digital literacy', UNESCO UIS '[Country] ICT skills', '[Country] digital literacy survey', World Bank Digital Development diagnostic.

items.title: Tertiary ICT/STEM Education
items.text: Field Value Variable ict-capacity-tertiaryict Definition The scale and quality of tertiary-level ICT and STEM education programmes available in the country, based on the number and quality of institutions offering ICT degrees, computer science programmes, and engineering courses relative to the population. Format Categorical: 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=Very limited (<2 institutions with ICT programmes); 2=Limited (few institutions, low enrolment); 3=Moderate (several institutions, growing enrolment); 4=Good (established institutions with diverse ICT programmes); 5=Strong (multiple quality institutions, high enrolment, regional reputation) Other Metadata Primary sources: UNESCO UIS education statistics (STEM graduates), QS World University Rankings by subject (Computer Science), national higher education authorities, ACM/IEEE accreditation records. Proxy: STEM graduates as % of total graduates from UNESCO. Data Collection Guidelines Search: UNESCO UIS '[Country] STEM graduates', QS Rankings '[Country] computer science', '[Country] universities ICT programmes', '[Country] computer science degree', national higher education council, Times Higher Education '[Country]'.

items.title: Developer and Tech Community
items.text: Field Value Variable ict-capacity-devcommunity Definition The size and vibrancy of the local software developer and technology professional community. Measured through proxies such as GitHub activity, Stack Overflow representation, local tech meetup groups, and developer surveys. Indicates the country's capacity to build and maintain digital systems locally. Format Categorical: 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=Negligible (very few active developers); 2=Emerging (small but growing community, <1,000 active developers); 3=Developing (active community, local meetups, 1,000-10,000 developers); 4=Established (vibrant community, multiple events, 10,000-50,000 developers); 5=Mature (large community, international visibility, 50,000+ developers) Other Metadata Primary sources: GitHub Octoverse (developer geography), Stack Overflow Developer Survey, Google Developer Groups directory, AfriLabs member hubs by country, Andela/developer training programme presence. Approximate based on multiple signals. Data Collection Guidelines Search: GitHub '[Country] developers', Google Developer Groups '[Country]', Stack Overflow '[Country]', '[Country] tech community', '[Country] software developers', AfriLabs '[Country]', '[Country] coding bootcamp'.

items.title: Gender Gap in ICT Access
items.text: Field Value Variable ict-capacity-gendergap Definition The gap between men and women in mobile internet usage or internet access. Based on the GSMA Mobile Gender Gap data or ITU gender-disaggregated ICT statistics. A smaller gap indicates more equitable access to digital opportunities. Format Categorical: 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=Very large gap (>40% fewer women online); 2=Large gap (30-40% fewer); 3=Moderate gap (20-29% fewer); 4=Small gap (10-19% fewer); 5=Near parity (<10% gap) Other Metadata Primary sources: GSMA Mobile Gender Gap Report (annual), ITU gender-disaggregated ICT data, Afrobarometer gender analysis, EQUALS Global Partnership. GSMA publishes gender gap data for most African countries. Data Collection Guidelines Search: GSMA Mobile Gender Gap Report '[Country]', GSMA Connected Women '[Country]', ITU '[Country] gender internet', Afrobarometer '[Country] gender digital', '[Country] women internet access'. The gender gap is usually expressed as a percentage difference.

items.title: E-Government Development
items.text: Field Value Variable ict-capacity-egovreadiness Definition The country's e-government development level based on the UN E-Government Development Index (EGDI). This composite index measures online service delivery, telecommunications infrastructure, and human capital. Indicates the government's capacity to deliver digital public services. Format Categorical: 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=EGDI <0.25 (Low); 2=EGDI 0.25-0.49 (Middle); 3=EGDI 0.50-0.64 (High); 4=EGDI 0.65-0.79 (Very High); 5=EGDI 0.80+ (Very High) Other Metadata Primary sources: UN E-Government Survey (published biennially, most recent 2024). Covers all UN member states including all 54 African countries. EGDI ranges 0 to 1. Data Collection Guidelines Search: UN E-Government Survey 2024 '[Country]', UN DESA E-Government Development Index '[Country]', publicadministration.un.org country data. The UN publishes individual country profiles with EGDI scores.

items.title: Tech Startup Ecosystem
items.text: Field Value Variable ict-innovation-startupecosystem Definition The maturity of the country's technology startup ecosystem, based on the number of tech startups, venture capital activity, accelerators/incubators, and the presence of a supportive ecosystem. Africa's startup scene is well-documented by trackers like Partech, Disrupt Africa, and Briter Bridges. Format Categorical: 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=Nascent (very few tech startups, no visible VC activity); 2=Emerging (some startups, limited funding, 1-2 hubs); 3=Developing (growing startup base, regular funding rounds, several hubs); 4=Established (significant startup activity, $50M+ annual VC, multiple hubs and accelerators); 5=Leading (major ecosystem, $200M+ annual VC, international recognition) Other Metadata Primary sources: Africa: The Big Deal (tracks every disclosed funding round), Partech Africa Tech Venture Capital Report, Disrupt Africa funding report, Briter Bridges ecosystem maps, GSMA DNSI tech startup indicators. Well-documented sector. Data Collection Guidelines Search: Africa: The Big Deal '[Country]' funding data, Partech Africa '[Country]', Disrupt Africa '[Country]', '[Country] tech startup ecosystem', '[Country] venture capital tech', Briter Bridges '[Country]'. Note total VC funding and major startups.

items.title: Technology and Innovation Hubs
items.text: Field Value Variable ict-innovation-techhubs Definition The number and activity level of technology hubs, incubators, accelerators, and co-working spaces focused on technology and innovation. These provide physical infrastructure and support for tech entrepreneurship and skills development. Format Categorical: 0=Unknown or n.a.; 1=None identified; 2=1-2 hubs with limited activity; 3=3-9 hubs with regular activity; 4=10-24 hubs with diverse focus areas; 5=25+ hubs with a vibrant, mature ecosystem Other Metadata Primary sources: AfriLabs network directory (largest pan-African hub network), Briter Bridges innovation map, GSMA Ecosystem Accelerator, national ICT authority directories. AfriLabs has mapped over 400 hubs across Africa. Data Collection Guidelines Search: AfriLabs directory '[Country]', Briter Bridges innovation map '[Country]', '[Country] tech hubs', '[Country] innovation hubs incubators', GSMA Ecosystem Accelerator '[Country]', i-Hub/CcHub/other known hub networks.

items.title: Global Innovation Index Ranking
items.text: Field Value Variable ict-innovation-gii Definition The country's performance on the WIPO Global Innovation Index (GII), which measures innovation capabilities across institutions, human capital, infrastructure, market sophistication, business sophistication, knowledge outputs, and creative outputs. Provides a composite view of the national innovation ecosystem. Format Categorical: 0=Unknown or n.a. (not included in GII); 1=GII rank 121+ or bottom quartile; 2=GII rank 101-120; 3=GII rank 81-100; 4=GII rank 61-80; 5=GII rank 1-60 Other Metadata Primary sources: WIPO Global Innovation Index (published annually). GII 2024 covers 133 economies including most African countries. Individual country profiles available on WIPO website with detailed sub-indicator breakdowns. Data Collection Guidelines Search: WIPO Global Innovation Index 2024 '[Country]', GII 2024 country profile, wipo.int Global Innovation Index rankings. Note overall rank, sub-index ranks for innovation inputs and outputs, and year-on-year change.
